diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 5771125..edb7f9d 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -103,2 +103,9 @@ Day 98 / Week 14 observations:
 - Usage peaked around 1.1M/day, still safely within tier 3. Open issues are rising but remain near one-day resolution; social response to higher B/C quotas was positive.
 - Week 14 adjustment: scale S2 content more assertively, add matching S2 support capacity, keep prices/dev/tiers stable, and save toward tier-2 R&D once cash exceeds the $333k cost with a buffer.
+
+Day 105 / Week 15 observations:
+- Cash rose to $417k, enough to fund tier-2 R&D while leaving some operating buffer. Last week's net was about +$152k before any one-time research cost.
+- S2 content scaled extremely well: 1,090 paid content subscribers from 3,955 leads, with 379 B and 654 C at strong prices.
+- Active mix is now centered on S2 B/C: 2,215 B and 2,766 C, while low-value S1/S2 A continues to shrink through churn/mix shift.
+- Usage peaked around 1.24M/day, still under tier-3 capacity. S2 issue aging is low, but S1 issue aging is creeping up; social is now mostly positive with an onboarding/documentation complaint.
+- Week 15 adjustment: start tier-2 R&D, hold the proven S2 growth/support/dev settings steady to rebuild cash after the one-time spend, and post about onboarding templates.
